{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.4": 3, "R1.7": 2, "RX.1": 4, "RX.3": 4, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 3},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "Mentions prior work on clarification-based retrieval and general domain IR but doesn't compare to specific recent works in clinical imaging or LLM-driven disambiguation.",
    "R1.7": "No explicit citations are provided, although the text alludes to related work in information retrieval and query disambiguation.",
    "RX.1": "Flags uncertainty by stating that the system triggers clarification on fewer than 8% of queries and that the evaluation is limited in scope.",
    "RX.3": "The abstract states that RadResolve was evaluated on 8,200 chest CT reports from a single academic center, indicating it covers the agreed-upon active set.",
    "RX.4": "No indication of harmful content or regressions is presented.",
    "RX.5": "The text describes the training data and the margin-augmented cross-entropy loss used, but doesn't provide specific details on how non-cosmetic changes are annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: Mentions prior work on clarification-based retrieval and general domain IR but doesn't compare to specific recent works in clinical imaging or LLM-driven disambiguation.
rationale.R1.7: No explicit citations are provided, although the text alludes to related work in information retrieval and query disambiguation.
rationale.RX.1: Flags uncertainty by stating that the system triggers clarification on fewer than 8% of queries and that the evaluation is limited in scope.
rationale.RX.3: The abstract states that RadResolve was evaluated on 8,200 chest CT reports from a single academic center, indicating it covers the agreed-upon active set.
rationale.RX.4: No indication of harmful content or regressions is presented.
rationale.RX.5: The text describes the training data and the margin-augmented cross-entropy loss used, but doesn't provide specific details on how non-cosmetic changes are annotated with source and reason.
